name: okta_login_from_blocked_country
description: A user successfully signed in from a country on the org's high-risk/blocked list (user.session.start with geo.country in a configured deny-list).

validation.notes: Honeycomb-native detection - there is no Panther Okta equivalent (Panther uses geo-velocity / 'New Geo-Location' behavior heuristics rather than a fixed country list). The deny-list is the set of countries with a country-wide U.S. State Department Level 4 'Do Not Travel' advisory, captured 2026-06-24 from https://travel.state.gov/en/international-travel/travel-advisories.html (23 countries: Afghanistan, Belarus, Burkina Faso, Burma/Myanmar, Central African Republic, Chad, D.R. Congo, Haiti, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Niger, North Korea, Russia, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Uganda, Ukraine, Yemen). Countries with a Level 4 advisory only for specific sub-national regions (e.g. Oman/Saudi Arabia Yemen-border, parts of Guatemala/Mexico) are intentionally excluded because geo.country is country-granularity. MAINTENANCE: advisory levels change - re-pull periodically. NAMING: geo.country values come from MaxMind (confirmed by 'Czechia'/'U.S. Virgin Islands' in live data), so the list uses MaxMind canonical names plus the two highest-value aliases (Burma+Myanmar, Russia+Russian Federation). Other MaxMind spellings could not be validated here (zero traffic from these countries in 90d) - confirm against live data if a match is ever expected to be missed. The 'in' operator was validated on geo.country against live low-volume countries (Egypt/Thailand/Singapore) and the full deny-list executes cleanly (0 hits/90d). LIMITATIONS: country granularity only; VPN/proxy egress can mask or fake the apparent country (pair with okta_anonymizing_vpn_login). Filtering to okta.outcome.result = SUCCESS targets completed logins; drop that filter to also catch blocked-country attempts.
